captureScope.reference: main, section, [role='main'] — live site selector; desktop captures include OS taskbar strip (~48px) at bottom
captureScope.current: [data-window-compare-stage] — Storybook window component only, no OS taskbar
captureScope.note: Scope mismatch exists in the taskbar strip region for all desktop states. This is capture infrastructure noise, not component drift. taskbar-clock-date mismatch is classified as a noiseSource, not a driftCategory.

results.driftSummary: Storybook search trigger shows '검색' text label absent in live. Sidebar expand indicators differ (▸/▾ text vs live indicator). Address bar renders folder icon + label vs live text-only. Taskbar strip and thumbnail differences are noise (not component drift).

Live chip overlay shows chip pills only (no search input row). Storybook overlay has search input ('포트폴리오') + chips below. Chip label set matches but layout differs. Address bar icon drift same as desktop-card. Taskbar strip and thumbnail differences are noise.

Mobile captures match 390×794 CSS px at 3× DPR = 1170×2382. High mismatch driven by different thumbnail images (noise). Address bar icon differs. Structural layout (2-col grid, no sidebar, no chip bar) matches between live and Storybook.

Live titlebar tab has salmon/pink background (#f9d0cf range) vs Storybook gray/white (bg-gray-100). Address bar in Storybook shows navigation arrows + URL text; live shows breadcrumb text only. Article cover image renders at different crop. Taskbar strip is capture-scope noise.

Same titlebar tab background drift as desktop-chrome. Live address bar transitions to edit-mode input (highlighted border, text cursor); Storybook uses controlled addressValue prop rendering. Live dropdown item label '2025를 보내며' matches COMPARE_ADDRESS_OPEN_DROPDOWN_ITEMS fixture. Dropdown item count matches (1 item). Taskbar strip is capture-scope noise.

Mobile captures match at 3× DPR. Salmon/pink titlebar tab drift same as desktop. Live article body scrolls further (full article text visible). Storybook ArticleContent fixture is shorter (~2 paragraphs vs full article) — that length difference is noise. Structural chrome matches (tab label, address breadcrumb, close button position).

thumbnail-images.reason: Storybook uses a single repeated repo-local thumbnail PNG. Live uses real article cover images. Not a component chrome concern.

entry-metadata-text.reason: Live uses real published dates/tags; Storybook uses fixture strings. Minor text rendering differences not related to component chrome.

taskbar-clock-date.reason: Capture scope mismatch: reference PNGs captured via 'main, section, [role=main]' include the OS taskbar strip (~48px) at the bottom. Current captures use [data-window-compare-stage] (window component only, no taskbar). This strip of pixel mismatch is infrastructure noise, not component drift.

article-body-text.reason: Storybook ArticleContent fixture is shorter than the live article body. Scroll-content length difference, not a chrome concern.

article-content-length.reason: Same as article-body-text — fixture length vs full live article. Not a component chrome concern.

phase5ActionItems.description: Browser titlebar tab background is white/gray in Storybook but salmon/pink on live. Review Browser component's titlebar color token to align with live site.
Storybook renders folder icon in address bar breadcrumb area. Live site does not show folder icon in the address bar. Remove or conditionalize the address icon in the toolbar.
Storybook search trigger shows '검색' text label. Live folder search trigger is an empty styled element (no visible text label). Remove text label from search trigger or set it to empty.

Storybook folder search overlay shows search input row ('포트폴리오' value) above chips. Live chip overlay shows only chip pills with no input row. Align overlay structure: either remove search input from overlay or make it absent by default.
Storybook uses ▸/▾ Unicode character as expand toggle indicator. Live uses a different visual indicator. Consider aligning the expand indicator character or using an icon.

Live address bar edit-mode renders a text input with highlighted border and cursor. Storybook controlled addressValue prop renders differently. Investigate whether dropdown positioning or input styling differs.
